title: F013 — Wheeler Participatory Universe

requiredObjects: central registry or shared protocol layer

composition.colorLogic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors
composition.labelPolicy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F013 — Wheeler Participatory Universe".
Purpose: The observer and universe are shown as mutually implicated through information, distinction, question, and response.

Manuscript context: Wheeler suggested that the universe may be participatory in nature. His famous phrase ``It from Bit'' proposed that information occupies a foundational role in reality. According to Wheeler, physical phenomena may emerge from acts of distinction, measurement, and information acquisition. The observer is not necessarily external to reality. The observer participates within reality. The universe becomes a self-observing system. F013 — Wheeler Participatory UniverseThe observer and universe are shown as mutually implicated through information, distinction, question, and response.F013-wheeler-participatory-universe Delayed Choice Experiments Wheeler's delayed-choice experiments further challenged classical intuitions. In these experiments, choices made after a particle has apparently begun its journey seem to influence how the experiment manifests. Although these results do not imply backward causation in any simplistic sense, they demonstrate that the relationship between measurement and reality is more subtle than classical models suggest. The distinction between past and future becomes less rigid. Observation appears woven into the fabric of events themselves.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: delayed-choice measurement, observer-dependent experimental arrangement, observer participation, federated systems architecture.
Required visual objects: photon path, beam splitter or choice point, detector A, detector B, observer or measurement station, observer axis or observation node, information flow arrows, nodes, links, central registry or shared protocol layer.
Recommended short labels: choice point, detector, observer, information, node, federation.

Composition: left-to-right experimental flow.
Camera/view: orthographic.
Visual hierarchy: source/emission → path or choice point → measurement/detector → observer/information interpretation.
Color logic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors.
Label policy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
